mission.title: cert-manager: CloudDNS: use metadata server to auth with CloudDNS API
mission.description: CloudDNS: use metadata server to auth with CloudDNS API. Requested by 18+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

**What happened**:
When creating an Issuer on a GKE cluster, and leaving out the serviceAccountSecretRef it fails to manipulate DNS with built in serviceAccount for the cluster.

steps.title: Apply the fix for CloudDNS: use metadata server to auth with CloudDNS API
steps.description:
this PR enables gcloud authentication via meta authentication api
```yaml
#!/bin/bash
CLUSTERNAME="test-cert-manager"
PROJECT="yourgcpproject"
EMAIL="youremail@email.com"
YOURGCPDOMAIN="example.com"

# Provision GKE
gcloud --project=${PROJECT} container clusters create ${CLUSTERNAME} \
        --zone=us-west1-b \
        --num-nodes 1 \
        --cluster-version 1.8.5-gke.0 \
        --scopes "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/ndev.clouddns.readwrite"

# Init Helm
kubectl create serviceaccount --namespace kube-system tiller
kubectl create clusterrolebinding tiller-cluster-rule \
        --clusterrole=cluster-admin \
        --serviceaccount=kube-system:tiller
helm ini
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "CloudDNS: use metadata server to auth with CloudDNS API" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: this PR enables gcloud authentication via meta authentication api
codeSnippets:
#!/bin/bash
CLUSTERNAME="test-cert-manager"
PROJECT="yourgcpproject"
EMAIL="youremail@email.com"
YOURGCPDOMAIN="example.com"

# Provision GKE
gcloud --project=${PROJECT} container clusters create ${CLUSTERNAME} \
        --zone=us-west1-b \
        --num-nodes 1 \
        --cluster-version 1.8.5-gke.0 \
        --scopes "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/ndev.clouddns.readwrite"

# Init Helm
kubectl create serviceaccount --namespace kube-system tiller
kubectl create clusterrolebinding tiller-cluster-rule \
        --clusterrole=cluster-admin \
        --serviceaccount=kube-system:tiller
helm init --service-account tiller

# Install Cert-Manager
helm install --name cert-manager --namespace kube-system contrib/charts/cert-manager

# Init Let's Encrypt as issuer
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVers

Events:
  Type     Reason                   Age                From                     Message
  ----     ------                   ----               ----                     -------
  Warning  ErrorCheckCertificate    13s (x7 over 14s)  cert-manager-controller  Error checking existing TLS certificate: secret "app-ssl-secret" not found
  Normal   PrepareCertificate       13s (x6 over 14s)  cert-manager-controller  Preparing certificate with issuer
  Normal   PresentChallenge         13s (x6 over 13s)  cert-manager-controller  Presenting dns-01 challenge for domain test-name.example.com
  Warning  ErrorPrepareCertificate  13s (x6 over 13s)  cert-manager-controller  Error preparing issuer for certificate: error presenting acme authorization for domain "test-name.example.com": error getting

Client Version: version.Info{Major:"1", Minor:"8", GitVersion:"v1.8.4", GitCommit:"9befc2b8928a9426501d3bf62f72849d5cbcd5a3", GitTreeState:"clean", BuildDate:"2017-11-20T05:28:34Z", GoVersion:"go1.8.3", Compiler:"gc", Platform:"darwin/amd64"}
Server Version: version.Info{Major:"1", Minor:"8+", GitVersion:"v1.8.5-gke.0", GitCommit:"2c2a807131fa8708abc92f3513fe167126c8cce5", GitTreeState:"clean", BuildDate:"2017-12-19T20:05:45Z", GoVersion:"go1.8.3b4", Compiler:"gc", Platform:"linux/amd64"}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
